message:
feat(lab): add trace normalization for canonical replay ordering

NormalizationResult struct for normalization outcomes:
- normalized: reordered trace events
- original_switches / normalized_switches: context switch counts
- algorithm: method used (greedy, beam search, etc.)
- switch_reduction() / switch_reduction_pct() helpers
- Display impl showing "N events: X → Y switches (Z% reduction)"

normalize_for_replay() function:
- Reorders trace events to minimize context switches
- Preserves all happens-before relationships
- Uses geodesic-based algorithms from trace module
- Produces canonical form suitable for deterministic comparison

traces_equivalent() comparator:
- Compares two traces for semantic equivalence
- Ignores superficial ordering differences
- Uses normalization to find canonical representations

Module documentation:
- Examples for normalize_for_replay and traces_equivalent usage
- Explains use cases: debugging, trace minimization, deterministic comparison

Module exports (mod.rs):
- Export NormalizationResult, normalize_for_replay, traces_equivalent

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
